authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model explicitly identified the D2 (1300–1400 AD) vs D1/D3/D4 (Roman) conflict and resolved it using document-specific evidence: contamination risk from medieval-tent storage (D3) and D4’s clean-sample repeat test labeled definitive (50–150 AD). It agreed with the high-authority curator’s Roman claim (D1) but did so via corroboration rather than default deference, treating D4 as most authoritative for the scientific date.
